response:
The fare $\eta$ depends on the distance $\xi$ where the base cost is ten yuan for the first four kilometers. Any distance beyond four kilometers adds two yuan per extra kilometer to the total charge. This relationship creates a linear function for distances greater than four units of travel. The spring of the day often brings many travelers to the airport for their flights.

A payment of thirty-eight yuan indicates the total calculated distance was twenty-three kilometers based on the rate structure. Since the actual road distance was fifteen kilometers the difference of eight kilometers must come from waiting time. Converting eight equivalent kilometers into minutes using the five minute per kilometer rule yields forty minutes of stops. The spring season sees increased traffic which often leads to such delays on the road. This maximum stop time ensures the final fare matches the amount paid by the passenger.
